title: athyrium asplenioides var. subtripinnatum
intro: Athyrium asplenioides var. subtripinnatum, also known as the subtripinnate lady fern, is a type of fern native to North America, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds and unique subtripinnate leaf structure. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The subtripinnate lady fern is said to have various health benefits, such as acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, and has been used to treat conditions like eczema and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant may include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the dried leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
